title: Personas as reversible forks of templates — track upstream like git
description: Today adoption copies a template into a personal persona and severs the link. Treat every adopted persona as a fork with a pointer to its source template plus schema_version. When upstream template advances (principles refined, new decision rules, error_handling fixes), the user sees a 'template update available' diff UI — accept or reject per hunk, or pin. This reframes the catalog from a one-shot seed into a living library that keeps improving for users who already adopted.
reasoning: The current model wastes the catalog's most valuable property: it is iteratively improved, but that improvement never reaches users who adopted v1. Forking semantics turn template quality work into compounding value — every rewrite lifts the fleet of adopted personas. Combined with a schema migration pipeline, this is how the catalog stops being 'install once, drift forever'.
